creator: remko@elvandar.org
text:
> confusing statement regarding newfs(8) on vinum(4) volumes. > > > diff -r -u handbook.orig/vinum-object-naming.html > handbook/vinum-object-naming.html > --- handbook.orig/vinum-object-naming.html 2008-03-22 05:43:54.000000000 > +0100 > +++ handbook/vinum-object-naming.html 2008-03-24 16:27:00.000000000 +0100 > @@ -157,15 +157,6 @@ > Vinum volume might be called class="FILENAME">/dev/gvinum/concat, a name which > has no relationship with a partition name.

> > -

Normally, href="http://www.FreeBSD.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=newfs&sektion=8"> -class="CITEREFENTRY"> class="REFENTRYTITLE">newfs(8) interprets > -the name of the disk and complains if it cannot understand it. For > example:

> - > -
> -# newfs
> /dev/gvinum/concat
> -newfs: /dev/gvinum/concat: can't figure out file system partition
> -
> - >

In order to create a file system on this volume, use href="http://www.FreeBSD.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=newfs&sektion=8"> class="CITEREFENTRY"> class="REFENTRYTITLE">newfs(8):

> I dont see the confusing part actually; I think this tells what we should know in case we encounter such a thing right I dont see any reference to vinum(4) or anything... Can you explain this a bit more? I think the change like this isn't needed. Thanks, remko -- /"\ Best regards, | remko@FreeBSD.org \ / Remko Lodder | remko@EFnet X http://www.evilcoder.org/ | / \ ASCII Ribbon Campaign | Against HTML Mail and News
